summary: Parallel review: FAIL (1 critical, 13 warning, 11 suggestion). Compound gate: PASS

findingsList.text: 🟡 `test/runbook-add-cli-command.test.mjs`:27 — `flow:` field validated for presence only via `/^flow:/m`; `flow: bogus` passes all 5 schema assertions — add an allowlist check against known flow keys (`light-review`, `build-verify`, `full-stack`) before the matching engine is implemented
The one 🟡 item (flow-value not allowlist-checked in tests) is a low-effort pre-engine backlog task; it does not block merge.

🟡 `test/runbook-add-cli-command.test.mjs:20` — Schema validated by raw-string regex, not YAML parsing. A structurally broken YAML passes all 5 assertions as long as field names appear in the text. Backlog: add a YAML parse step before field assertions.

🟡 `test/runbook-dir.test.mjs:34` — Asserts `runbooks` `mkdirSync` appears after `features` `mkdirSync` by string index in source text (`src.indexOf(...)`). Tests source layout, not runtime behavior. Any valid refactor of `init.mjs` breaks this test without breaking functionality. Backlog: replace with behavioral filesystem assertion.

**Summary:** The primary deliverable is correct. The runbook YAML is clean (no speculative fields), `flow: build-verify` maps to a live constant in `flows.mjs:31`, both `mkdirSync` scaffolding calls are idempotent (`{ recursive: true }`), and all 4 tasks have substantive hints. The two 🟡 findings are test fragility issues — not functional defects. Neither blocks merge but both should be backlogged before the runbook engine is built.

🟡 `.team/features/runbook-system/tasks/task-1/` — No `artifacts/test-output.txt` captured. Handshake lists 3 artifacts but no test evidence is saved. Future audits can't verify without re-running. Backlog: save test output as part of builder handshake.
🟡 Gate output (provided) — Truncated; ends before `runbook-add-cli-command.test.mjs` results appear. Cannot confirm pass/fail from gate capture alone. Investigate gate output truncation.

**Requirement met:** File exists, valid schema, exactly 4 tasks with `title` + `hint`. The prior Simplicity FAIL (gold-plated fields) was resolved in run_2 before this review. No blocking findings. The two 🟡 warnings are process gaps for backlog.

🟡 `test/runbook-add-cli-command.test.mjs:20` — schema validated via raw regex, not YAML parsing; a structurally broken YAML file (bad indentation, duplicate keys) still passes all 5 assertions; add a `YAML.parse()` roundtrip before field checks

🟡 `test/runbook-dir.test.mjs:26` — tests `init.mjs` source code text, not runtime behavior; a valid refactor extracting `mkdirSync` to a helper breaks the test without breaking functionality; replace with an integration assertion on the resulting filesystem state

- The two 🟡 findings are test quality problems, not functional defects. Both become meaningful when the runbook engine is built and these tests become the regression guard. They should be backlogged now rather than discovered then.

🟡 `.team/runbooks/add-cli-command.yml:3-13` — `patterns` + `minScore` define a scoring/matching system with zero consumers; no code reads any runbook YAML file (confirmed by exhaustive grep — only `mkdirSync` references exist). Aspirational schema for a matcher not yet built. Does not hit the gold-plating veto threshold because "valid schema" was the stated task requirement, but defer adding these fields to further runbook files until the matcher is implemented.

🟡 `test/runbook-dir.test.mjs:34-41` — Asserts source-code ordering of two `mkdirSync` calls by text index in `init.mjs`. Tests layout, not behavior. A valid refactor of `init.mjs` (extracting dirs to an array, reordering) silently breaks this test without breaking runtime behavior. Replace with a filesystem assertion after running `agt init`.

No 🔴 vetoes. The prior Simplicity FAIL (four speculative tracking fields: `tier`, `createdAt`, `usageCount`, `lastUsedAt`) was cleanly resolved by run_2. What remains is a minimal, focused change: one YAML data file, two test files, two `mkdirSync` one-liners. The two warnings should go to the backlog before the runbook engine is built.
